headlines.title: 11 presumed dead in Washington state paper mill implosion as rescue shifts to recovery
headlines.description: Washington was bracing for the state’s ‘deadliest industrial tragedy,’ Gov. Bob Ferguson said Wednesday following the rupture of a tank at the Nippon Dynawave plant.

headlines.title: James Talarico says he ‘missed the mark’ on ‘cringey’ comments as Texas general election starts
headlines.description: Texas state Rep. James Talarico said Wednesday that previous comments he’s made on sensitive cultural issues “missed the mark,” as Republicans lean on those remarks to attack him in the contentious Senate race.

headlines.title: Trump racks up May primary wins in Republican retribution campaign
headlines.description: President Donald Trump went on a winning streak in May primaries as he ousted several Republicans who had dissented from his party line, further tightening his grip on the GOP.

headlines.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing 300 gold bars in his house
headlines.description: A former CIA officer is accused of stashing more than 300 gold bars in his house, worth $40 million, as well as lying about his educational background. The man claimed he needed the gold bars for “work-related expenses,” according to court documents and two people familiar with his employment history. NBC News' Julia Ainsley breaks down the details in the exclusive story.

headlines.title: Insurance denies coverage for teen’s surgery to treat severe epilepsy
headlines.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports on a 13-year-old Illinois girl who suffers from as many as four seizures a day. Insurance denied coverage for the surgery her doctor said could help, but then reversed its decision days after we asked about the case.

headlines.title: U.S. plans to send Americans exposed to Ebola to facility in Kenya
headlines.description: The Trump administration is preparing a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who have been exposed to Ebola amid the escalating outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, an administration official said Wednesday

headlines.title: Four years after World Cup drama, Gio Reyna gets another shot with Team USA
headlines.description: Last week, Gio Reyna and his wife, Chloe, were sitting in a parking lot, outside a smoothie shop in Connecticut, waiting to hear if he’d made Team USA’s World Cup roster.

headlines.title: Red-hot AI demand pushes more companies into the $1 trillion club
headlines.description: Micron Technologies and SK Hynix joined the trillion-dollar club, which includes Apple, Meta and Tesla.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Jill Biden says she thought Joe Biden was ‘having a stroke’ during 2024 debate
headlines.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said in an interview clip released Wednesday that she thought her husband, former President Joe Biden, was “having a stroke” during his critical June 2024 debate, which catalyzed the chain of events that eventually led him to drop out of the presidential election.“I don’t know what happened.

headlines.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.
headlines.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.

headlines.title: Illinois Legislature passes historic AI bill that would require third-party safety audits
headlines.description: The measure, SB315, goes beyond legislation from California and New York regulating America’s largest AI companies. It still needs to be signed by the governor.
